insight: Inflation in Prismata measures the ROI (return on investment) players are getting from their purchases. Pre-absorb, inflation is approximately 1.3; post-absorb, it rises to approximately 1.4; and in late-game desperate defense scenarios (opponents holding Drones or Rhinos), it can rise significantly higher. Attackers generate high ROI because each point of attack forces increasingly costly defensive responses from the opponent.
author: 307th
context: 307th explaining the Prismata inflation concept to jrkirby, who asked for a better explanation of the 'value vs inflation' charts.

insight: Inflation understanding helps determine the optimal turn to transition from economy-building to attacking. Early game favors drone investment (inflation ~1.3), but at some threshold it becomes correct to switch to the higher-return attack curve (~1.4+). Understanding this mathematically or intuitively helps identify that transition point.
author: ussgordoncaptain
context: Discussion between 307th and ussgordoncaptain on the practical application of inflation math for deciding when to start building attackers.

insight: The Prismata rating system ('eloyot') is not standard ELO; it has been modified so that wins and losses against much lower-rated players result in less score change than in a standard ELO system. Elyot confirmed the rating differences and win rates are somewhat analogous to ELO but the system handles large rating gaps differently.
author: steel0229e
context: Community debate about ELO inflation and whether current high ratings reflect past skill levels; steel referenced an Elyot blog post about the rating system change.

insight: Elyot confirmed that Chill was added as a mechanic because it was found to have an unexpectedly high number of interesting strategic consequences relative to its rules complexity, fitting the design goal of maximizing insightful strategic concepts per unit of Kolmogorov complexity. Lifespan and Stamina were kept as separate mechanics because chilling lifespan-1 units is strategically important enough to warrant the distinction.
author: elyot
context: Elyot explaining the design philosophy behind mechanic choices, responding to community discussion about whether Chill adds too much complexity.

insight: The Master Bot can be beaten even when given a 5-gold handicap in some sets, and steel0229e notes that in certain sets you can beat Master Bot by skipping the first turn entirely, suggesting the bot has set-dependent exploitable weaknesses significant enough to overcome large handicaps.
author: steel0229e
context: Discussion about whether the 'Unfair Challenge' (bot gets an extra drone) and the 5-gold-handicap achievement are still beatable.

insight: The achievement requiring the player to beat Master Bot while giving it a 5-gold handicap must be done with fully random units (honorable game requirement); players cannot pick a unit set specifically designed to exploit bot weaknesses. The achievement is considered very doable by playing efficiently.
author: iminabearsuit
context: awaclus asking whether the 5-gold handicap MB achievement could be gamed by choosing a set the bot is weak against; iminabearsuit confirmed random units are required.

insight: Top expert apooche attributes improvement over roughly 18 months primarily to increased in-game knowledge of unit interactions and builds rather than mechanical skill, and credits the in-client analysis tool as a major contributor to that learning. He also notes that bad habits (like locking in turns without planning next-turn purchases) are separable from raw skill level.
author: apooche
context: Apooche reflecting on his own improvement and rating increase from 2100 to 2200, and what drove that growth.

insight: In Prismata community discussion, 'inflation' as applied to unit value charts refers to the in-game economic concept of investment ROI, not to the rating system. A unit with 'better value at low inflation' is stronger in the early game when drone investment dominates, while a unit better at high inflation is stronger late-game when attack ROI surpasses economy ROI.
author: 307th
context: jrkirby asked for clarification on how to read 'value vs inflation' charts shown for units in community resources.

insight: Multiple top experts including elyot noted that Vivid Drone and Deadeye Operative received unit changes around 2016-2018; these are cited as examples of the few gameplay changes in that period alongside cosmetic/feature additions.
author: supergreyman
context: Simon Sapsaus returning after 1.5 years asking what changed; Greyman summarized the unit changes that occurred.
